---
title: "Technical Supplementary for the Manuscript"
author: "Linh Dang"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Removing trivial noise taxa in contaminant survey samples

``` r
# pseq is phyloseq object created from count table
require(phyloseq)
require(microViz)
pseq %>% 
  microViz::tax_filter(prev_detection_threshold = 2,
                       min_prevalence = 2,
                       min_total_abundance = 1e-6)
```

## Removing trivial noise taxa in murine PDAC study

``` r
# pseq_true: intratumor PDAC sample created from count table
pseq_true <- pseq_true %>% 
  microViz::tax_filter(min_prevalence = 1,
                       prev_detection_threshold = 2,
                       min_total_abundance = 1e-6)
# pseq_NCT: negative control corresponding for the study
pseq_NCT <- pseq_NCT %>% 
  microViz::tax_filter(min_prevalence = 1,
                       prev_detection_threshold = 2,
                       min_total_abundance = 5e-7)
```

## Composite Score

Let $\mathbb{C}$ be the putative contaminant set derived from the NCT survey, and $\mathbb{D}$ be a set of taxa remained after a certain decontamination method. To be rigorous, we only consider taxa whose prevalence greater or equal 50% as putative true taxa in $\mathbb{D}$, denoted this set as $\mathbb{D}^{'}$. The formula for the metric yield and purity as follows:

$$
Purity = \frac{|\mathbb{D}^{'} \setminus \mathbb{C}|}{|\mathbb{D}^{'}|}
$$

$$
Yield = \frac{|\mathbb{D}^{'} \setminus \mathbb{C}|}{|\mathbb{D}|}
$$ Thus, the composite score is: $$
Composite_score = Purity \times Yield = \frac{|\mathbb{D}^{'} \setminus \mathbb{C}|^{2}}{|\mathbb{D}^{'}| \times |\mathbb{D}|}
$$
